title: Innovative Springy Greek Lentils Dolmades
description: A herbal and springy one-pot Greek dish featuring brown lentils, this recipe is perfect for a healthy and flavorful dinner, and the dolmades form adds a fun twist to traditional lentil dishes.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the brown lentils, chopped fresh dill, chopped fresh parsley, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Stir to combine and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
While the lentils are cooking, prepare the grape leaves by rinsing them in cold water and removing the stems.
To assemble the dolmades, lay a grape leaf flat on a work surface, with the stem end facing you.
Place about 1 tablespoon of the lentil mixture in the center of the grape leaf.
Fold the stem end over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the dolmade into a neat package.
Repeat with the remaining grape leaves and lentil mixture.
Serve the dolmades warm, garnished with additional chopped fresh parsley if desired.
